title: Borders, shadows, gradients and backgrounds

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling draft for Borders, shadows, gradients and backgrounds; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CSS Snapshot 2024; W3C Working Group Note 2024; provisions applicable to Borders, shadows, gradients and backgrounds
sources.claim: Background layers paint behind content and can combine color, images, gradients, size, position, and repeat without adding semantic content.

sources.locator: MDN CSS Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Borders, shadows, gradients and backgrounds
sources.claim: Background layers paint behind content and can combine color, images, gradients, size, position, and repeat without adding semantic content.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Background layers paint behind content and can combine color, images, gradients, size, position, and repeat without adding semantic content.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A decorative gradient sits over a solid fallback color while all instructional text remains real HTML. Your goal is to implement a visual surface treatment that degrades safely and preserves readability. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Lengths, percentages and viewport units; html semantics text; Color and contrast; Content, padding, border and margin. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Background layers paint behind content and can combine color, images, gradients, size, position, and repeat without adding semantic content. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Background images can fail, disappear in forced colors, increase contrast problems, and should not carry indispensable information. This draft therefore rejects: A background image is an appropriate place for required instructions because it cannot be selected.

includes: Use decorative layers without obscuring content, focus or contrast.
Implement a visual surface treatment that degrades safely and preserves readability.
excludes: Background images can fail, disappear in forced colors, increase contrast problems, and should not carry indispensable information.

outcomes: Implement a visual surface treatment that degrades safely and preserves readability.

examples.title: Borders, shadows, gradients and backgrounds: concrete case
examples.situation: A decorative gradient sits over a solid fallback color while all instructional text remains real HTML.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A decorative gradient sits over a solid fallback color while all instructional text remains real HTML.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Keep essential information in semantic content and use backgrounds decoratively with resilient color and contrast fallbacks.
examples.result: The result follows because Keep essential information in semantic content and use backgrounds decoratively with resilient color and contrast fallbacks.

examples.title: Borders, shadows, gradients and backgrounds: boundary case
examples.situation: Background images can fail, disappear in forced colors, increase contrast problems, and should not carry indispensable information.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Background images can fail, disappear in forced colors, increase contrast problems, and should not carry indispensable information.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: A background image is an appropriate place for required instructions because it cannot be selected.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Keep essential information in semantic content and use backgrounds decoratively with resilient color and contrast fallbacks.

analogy.example: A layered stage design can model Borders, shadows, gradients and backgrounds, where rules size, place, and paint existing content.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Borders, shadows, gradients and backgrounds before using exact platform terms
limitations: Physical layers do not reproduce cascade algorithms, intrinsic sizing, writing modes, or user preferences.
